details.body: Auto-populated plan_hash from snapshot can cause false-positive rejection on terminal replay after plan evolution. Scenario: (1) bead completed with plan_hash=None, auto-populated to snapshot's hash_v1; (2) new plan persisted, snapshot.plan_hash becomes hash_v2; (3) terminal replay of same completion with plan_hash=None auto-populates to hash_v2; (4) `option_conflicts(Some(hash_v1), Some(hash_v2))` at fs.rs:3161 triggers an `incompatible_terminal_update` error, breaking idempotent replay. Pre-change, the replay would pass None through to the adapter where `option_conflicts(Some(x), None)` is always false. Fix: either skip auto-population in `update_task_run` when the entry is already terminal (requires a two-phase check or passing the original caller value alongside the effective value), or weaken the terminal replay conflict check to treat plan_hash mismatch as non-fatal when the stored entry already has a plan_hash (backfill-only semantics, matching run_id behavior).

`src/adapters/fs.rs:2993-3023` still does not use `started_at` when it falls back from a supplied `run_id` to a single open runless row. As written, any named completion with the right bead/project will finalize that row even if the caller's `started_at` points at a different attempt. That violates the service contract in `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:466-469` and can backfill the wrong legacy attempt. Require `entries[*index].started_at == started_at` before accepting the runless fallback, and add a regression test that a mismatched timestamp is rejected.

`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:429-441` and `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:564-576` now replace every omitted `plan_hash` with `snapshot.plan_hash` before the lineage layer matches an existing row. Because `persist_plan` can later advance `snapshot.plan_hash` (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:347-385`), an idempotent start/completion replay against a row that already carries the older plan hash will now trip the conflict checks in `src/adapters/fs.rs:2711-2725` or `src/adapters/fs.rs:3159-3201`. The fallback needs to happen only when the matched row lacks `plan_hash`, not blindly at the service boundary; please add replay coverage across a later plan update.

`plan_hash` backfill on existing open rows can stamp the wrong plan version after a later `persist_plan()`. In `src/adapters/fs.rs:2914-2919` and `src/adapters/fs.rs:3246-3249`, replaying a start/completion for an existing row with `entry.plan_hash == None` pulls the hash from the current snapshot. But `persist_plan()` can advance `snapshot.plan_hash` at any time (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:365-376`), so a legacy attempt that originally ran under plan v1 can be relabeled as v2 if the plan changed before the replay. The new tests only cover rows that already had the original hash stored. Add a regression test for a missing-hash legacy row across plan evolution, and avoid snapshot-based backfill unless the original plan version is known.

The terminal-replay repair path still does not auto-populate `plan_hash` when the finalized row already exists without one. `update_task_run()` routes terminal replays through `backfill_terminal_entry()` (`src/adapters/fs.rs:3218-3224`), but `backfill_terminal_entry()` only copies a caller-supplied hash (`src/adapters/fs.rs:2670-2689`). If a legacy finalized row is replayed with `plan_hash=None`, the repair write can fill `run_id`, `outcome_detail`, and `finished_at` while leaving `plan_hash` unset, so amendment 3 is still incomplete for repair flows. Add a regression test with a preseeded terminal row whose `plan_hash` is missing and the caller omits it.

The round-trip struct-drift tests (`start_journal_details_round_trips_with_model_render` and `completion_journal_details_round_trips_with_model_render`) populate all optional fields with `Some` values, which correctly catches missing fields via the re-render equality assertion. However, neither `StartJournalDetails` nor `CompletionJournalDetails` uses `#[serde(deny_unknown_fields)]`. This means that in production (outside the test), if a new field is added to the model's render function but not to the fs adapter's parsing struct, parsing will silently succeed and `merge()` will silently discard the unrecognized field — the exact failure mode Issue 1 describes. The round-trip test catches this in CI, but adding `#[serde(deny_unknown_fields)]` to both `StartJournalDetails` and `CompletionJournalDetails` would also catch it at runtime, providing defense-in-depth. If forward-compatibility with newer journal formats is a concern, this could be gated behind a test-only assertion instead.

details.message: backend invocation failed for contract 'plan_and_implement' via 'claude': schema validation failed for stage 'plan_and_implement': invalid type: map, expected a string

details.body: snapshot_plan_hash() silently swallows I/O errors (corrupt snapshot JSON, permission failures) via .ok(), making them indistinguishable from 'no snapshot exists.' Consider logging a warning or tracing event when read_snapshot returns an Err that is not NotFound, so operators can detect a corrupted snapshot that is silently preventing plan_hash auto-population.

src/adapters/fs.rs:2279-2339 still duplicates the journal detail schema instead of reusing the canonical payload types in src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:353-386, and the new regression tests at src/adapters/fs.rs:3768-3812 only round-trip one concrete payload. That does not guarantee drift detection: a future optional or conditionally serialized field can be added to the model payload without affecting these fixtures, so the duplicated fs structs still compile and the tests still pass. Amendment 1 asked for drift to fail at compile/test time; this guard is still example-based rather than structural.

`update_task_run` still accepts the wrong finalized runless attempt when the caller later supplies a `run_id`. In `src/adapters/fs.rs:3013-3017`, the no-exact-match path falls back to `unique_terminal_replay_match`, and that helper (`src/adapters/fs.rs:2756-2788`) never checks `started_at` for runless terminal rows. A replay of a legacy finalized attempt with the wrong `started_at` will therefore backfill `run_id` onto that terminal row instead of rejecting it, even though Amendment 2 required `started_at` to disambiguate runless-to-named backfills. Add the same `started_at` guard for terminal fallback that you added for open matches, and cover it with a regression test.

The journal-detail drift fix is not complete yet. `StartJournalDetails` and `CompletionJournalDetails` are now shared model structs (`src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:353-394`), but the adapter still hand-merges only the current optional fields in `src/adapters/fs.rs:2284-2305` and `src/adapters/fs.rs:2322-2354`. If a new field is added to the shared structs later, the code will still compile and the new round-trip tests will still pass, but `append_event_if_missing` will silently drop that field during journal repair because `merge` never copies it. Add a merge-coverage test or refactor the merge logic so future field additions fail loudly instead of truncating repaired events.

In `json_fill_none` (fs.rs:2284-2300), the `entry().or_insert(value)` merge silently prefers base over overlay for ALL fields, including any future optional fields that may need conflict detection. Currently, conflict checks for run_id, plan_hash, and outcome_detail are performed explicitly before calling `json_fill_none`, but if a future optional field with conflict semantics is added to StartJournalDetails or CompletionJournalDetails, the merge will silently keep the stale base value without flagging the conflict. Add a code comment at the `json_fill_none` call sites (fs.rs:2326 and fs.rs:2367) documenting the invariant: any new optional field with conflict semantics MUST have an explicit conflict check added to `merge()` above the `json_fill_none` call, otherwise conflicting values will be silently preserved from the existing record rather than flagged as an error.

The `json_fill_none` function (fs.rs:2284-2300) silently converts serialization/deserialization errors into `None`, which is indistinguishable from a structural merge rejection. In the `StartJournalDetails::merge` and `CompletionJournalDetails::merge` callers, this means a serde bug (e.g., a future field type that fails to round-trip through `serde_json::Value`) would appear as a rejected merge, causing duplicate journal events to be appended instead of being merged. Consider returning `Result<Option<T>, ...>` or at minimum adding a `tracing::warn` inside `json_fill_none` when `to_value` or `from_value` fails, so operators can distinguish serde failures from legitimate merge rejections.

`#[serde(deny_unknown_fields)]` on `StartJournalDetails` and `CompletionJournalDetails` (model.rs:357, 383) prevents parsing journal entries that contain fields added by a newer code version. If the binary is rolled back after new-format entries are written, `parse()` returns `None`, journal merge is skipped, and duplicate events accumulate. This is data-safe but operationally surprising. Add a comment documenting this forward-compatibility tradeoff next to the `deny_unknown_fields` attribute, or switch to a lenient parse for existing journal entries (only use `deny_unknown_fields` in the round-trip test assertions where it actually catches drift).

details.message: backend invocation failed for contract 'plan_and_implement' via 'claude': schema validation failed for stage 'plan_and_implement': missing field `change_summary`

details.body: In `unique_terminal_replay_match` (fs.rs:2823-2828), when `requested_run_id` is `Some` but `requested_started_at` is `None`, a runless terminal candidate is accepted without any temporal guard. This can only happen from the call site at line 3194-3199 (the no-run_id path), which passes `None` for both — so it is currently safe. However, the function signature allows callers to pass `(Some(run_id), None)`, which would bypass the started_at guard entirely and permit backfilling the wrong legacy terminal row. Add a debug_assert or doc-comment contract stating that when `requested_run_id` is `Some`, `requested_started_at` should also be `Some` to enforce the invariant, or make the guard unconditional (reject all runless terminal candidates when `requested_started_at` is `None` and `requested_run_id` is `Some`).

The `json_fill_none` function (fs.rs:2289-2318) uses `entry(key).or_insert(value)` which means overlay values are only inserted when the key is completely absent from the base map. Since the structs use `skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none"`, a `None` field is omitted from the base map (key absent), and a `Some` value from the overlay will correctly fill it. However, if a future field is added without `skip_serializing_if` (e.g., a field that serializes to `null`), the base map WILL contain the key (with a `null` value) and the overlay's non-null value will NOT fill it. Add a test that explicitly verifies `json_fill_none` fills a `None` optional field when the overlay has a `Some` value, and document that all new `Option` fields on these structs MUST use `#[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]` for `json_fill_none` to work correctly.

The `debug_assert!` in `unique_terminal_replay_match` (fs.rs:2824) only fires in debug builds. In release builds, calling `(Some(run_id), None)` silently bypasses the started_at guard on runless terminal candidates, allowing backfill onto the wrong legacy row. Replace `debug_assert!` with a runtime check that returns `None` (or panics) to enforce the contract in all build modes.

The plan_hash auto-population from snapshot in `record_task_run_start` (fs.rs:3017-3019) runs unconditionally for new entries, but the comment block at fs.rs:2988-2992 explains that snapshot-based backfill is intentionally NOT applied to existing rows because persist_plan() can advance the snapshot hash after the original attempt started. However, the same race applies to new entries: if a plan is persisted between the bead being dispatched and `record_task_run_start` being called, the new entry gets stamped with the newer plan hash even though the bead was dispatched under the older plan. Consider documenting this known limitation in the code comment, or having the caller always supply plan_hash explicitly when it knows which plan version dispatched the bead.

src/adapters/fs.rs:2976-2991 and src/adapters/fs.rs:3297-3319 still leave `plan_hash` unset on existing lineage rows when callers omit it. New rows use `snapshot_plan_hash()`, but replaying or finalizing a preexisting row only copies an explicit caller-supplied `plan_hash` and then explicitly skips snapshot backfill. A legacy or partial row created with `plan_hash=None` therefore stays unlabeled forever even when the milestone snapshot already has the canonical hash, so issue 3 is still unimplemented for repair/replay paths. Please add a safe backfill path for existing rows as well, or persist enough provenance to distinguish plan evolution before deciding to skip it.

src/adapters/fs.rs:3059-3098 calls `unique_terminal_replay_match()` with every row for the same bead/project, and src/adapters/fs.rs:2859-2862 only accepts a candidate if every other row is a legacy start pair for that candidate. That makes named terminal replays fail as soon as the same bead/project has any other completed attempt: a runless terminal row for attempt A cannot be repaired/backfilled after later attempt B exists, even when `started_at` uniquely identifies attempt A. Filter the terminal fallback by `started_at` (or otherwise exclude unrelated attempts) before invoking the uniqueness check.

In `safe_plan_hash_backfill` (fs.rs:2628-2649), when the entry was created BEFORE any plan was persisted, both `plan_hash` and `snapshot_plan_hash_at_creation` are `None`. If a plan is later persisted and then the entry is revisited (via `update_task_run` or re-`record_task_run_start`), the backfill correctly refuses because `snapshot_plan_hash_at_creation` is `None` (legacy guard). However, this means entries created in a no-plan-yet state can NEVER be auto-populated from the snapshot, even though the system now knows which plan is executing. The acceptance criterion says 'Lineage entries auto-populate plan_hash from the milestone snapshot when callers omit it' — this edge case (start before plan, complete after plan) is not covered. Consider: when `snapshot_plan_hash_at_creation` is `None` AND `plan_hash` is `None` AND the entry is still Running (not terminal), it is safe to populate both `plan_hash` and `snapshot_plan_hash_at_creation` from the current snapshot during revisitation, since no prior plan version existed to conflict with.

src/adapters/fs.rs:3002-3030 finds an existing running attempt via the collapsed canonical view, but then repairs only the first raw row satisfying same_attempt. In duplicate-row states, that first raw row can be the stale copy with plan_hash=None while another duplicate already has the populated plan_hash/snapshot_plan_hash_at_creation. Replaying record_bead_start with plan_hash omitted then returns and writes the stale row unchanged, so the lineage file and BeadStarted journal never get repaired even though the canonical attempt already had the missing metadata. Repair from the merged canonical entry, or pick the most complete duplicate, before deciding there is no change.

src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:320-337 does not merge snapshot_plan_hash_at_creation, even though duplicate cleanup in src/adapters/fs.rs:3308-3315 and canonical collapsing in src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:630-644 both depend on merge_attempt_entries. If the surviving primary row is the stale duplicate and the only provenance-bearing copy is in a duplicate being removed, the merge drops that provenance permanently. After that, safe_plan_hash_backfill (src/adapters/fs.rs:2628-2648) can never recover plan_hash for that attempt, so the new issue-3 repair silently stops working on duplicate/replay paths.

details.message: backend invocation failed for contract 'plan_and_implement' via 'claude': Claude returned empty result with no structured_output (contract: plan_and_implement, stdout_len: 904, session_policy: ReuseIfAllowed)

details.body: In `safe_plan_hash_backfill` (fs.rs ~line 2636), the `Some(creation_hash)` branch does not check `entry.outcome.is_terminal()` before backfilling `plan_hash`. The `None` provenance branch at line 2653 correctly guards against terminal entries, but the `Some` branch is missing the same guard. Per Issue 3 spec: 'Terminal replay: Do NOT auto-populate plan_hash for terminal replays.' If a terminal entry has `snapshot_plan_hash_at_creation = Some(X)` and `plan_hash = None` (reachable through merge paths or preseeded data), and the snapshot hasn't evolved, the function will incorrectly set `plan_hash = Some(X)`. Fix: add `if entry.outcome.is_terminal() { return false; }` at the top of the `Some(creation_hash)` branch (or move the existing terminal guard before the match, so it applies to both branches). Add a regression test: preseed a terminal entry with `snapshot_plan_hash_at_creation = Some(hash)` and `plan_hash = None`, replay with `plan_hash = None`, assert `plan_hash` stays `None`.

Forward-compatible journal repair is still not implemented. `StartJournalDetails` and `CompletionJournalDetails` remain `#[serde(deny_unknown_fields)]` in `src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:377` and `src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:412`, so an older binary cannot parse journal details written by a newer binary and `append_event_if_missing` falls through to appending a duplicate event instead of repairing the existing row. The merge path in `src/adapters/fs.rs:2338-2407` also still relies on the generic `json_fill_none(...)` helper, so any future field with conflict semantics can be silently mishandled unless every caller is manually updated. Amendment 1 required forward-compatible deserialization plus explicit field-by-field merge rules; that gap remains.

Terminal replays can still synthesize a `plan_hash` on already-finalized rows. `TaskRunEntry::merge_attempt_entries()` now copies `snapshot_plan_hash_at_creation` onto the merged row (`src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:328-330`), and `update_task_run()` still calls `safe_plan_hash_backfill()` inside the already-finalized replay branch (`src/adapters/fs.rs:3371-3379`). Because `safe_plan_hash_backfill()` fills any row whose `snapshot_plan_hash_at_creation` matches the current snapshot (`src/adapters/fs.rs:2636-2642`), replaying a terminal completion with `plan_hash=None` can mutate a historical terminal row from `None` to the snapshot hash. The accepted contract for Amendment 3 was the opposite: terminal replays must preserve the caller-supplied `None`, and only open-row repairs may auto-populate `plan_hash`.

details.message: backend invocation failed for contract 'plan_and_implement' via 'claude': Claude returned empty result with no structured_output (contract: plan_and_implement, stdout_len: 905, session_policy: ReuseIfAllowed)

details.body: In `unique_terminal_replay_match` (fs.rs ~line 2837), the `debug_assert!` was replaced with a silent `return None`. The issue spec requires 'runtime checks that return errors in both debug and release builds.' While callers do produce an error when `None` is returned, the specific contract violation reason (run_id supplied without started_at) is lost, making debugging harder. Add a `tracing::error!` or `tracing::warn!` log before returning `None` so the contract violation is diagnosable in production logs, or change the return type to `Result<Option<usize>>` and return an explicit error.

`TaskRunEntry::merge_attempt_entries` (model.rs ~line 320) uses the same manual field-by-field pattern that was identified as drift-prone for journal details (issue 1), but has no compile-time or test-time guard against omitted fields. Add a round-trip coverage test analogous to `journal_merge_fills_all_optional_fields`: construct a primary with all optional fields None and a secondary with all fields populated, merge, serialize both the merged entry and the secondary, and assert the optional-field subset matches. This would catch a forgotten merge line when a new optional field is added to `TaskRunEntry`.

src/adapters/fs.rs:3034-3055 still records the current snapshot hash on brand-new `TaskRunEntry` rows whenever the caller omits `plan_hash`. The comment at lines 3037-3042 already describes the bug: if `persist_plan` runs after dispatch but before `record_task_run_start`, the start row is stamped with the newer snapshot hash even though the bead was launched under the older plan. That leaves permanently incorrect lineage and journal data for the attempt, so the new-row auto-population path is not actually safe.

src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:286-290 documents `snapshot_plan_hash_at_creation == None` as "legacy entries that predate this field", but src/adapters/fs.rs:2608-2615 treats `None` as proof that the row was created before any plan existed and immediately backfills the current snapshot hash. For real legacy data, those are not equivalent: an older open row created under plan V1 will also have `snapshot_plan_hash_at_creation=None`, so replaying or completing it after the snapshot advances to V2 will silently relabel the attempt with V2. The new provenance guard therefore does not protect existing lineages from plan-evolution corruption.

details.message: backend invocation failed for contract 'plan_and_implement' via 'claude': Claude returned empty result with no structured_output (contract: plan_and_implement, stdout_len: 923, session_policy: ReuseIfAllowed)

details.body: Issue 3 fix deviates from spec: The spec says 'Auto-populate plan_hash from the milestone snapshot' for new rows when caller's plan_hash is None, and backfill existing open entries from the snapshot. The implementation does the OPPOSITE — it explicitly removes auto-population (line 3039: `let effective_plan_hash = plan_hash.map(str::to_owned)`) and only records `snapshot_plan_hash_at_creation` as provenance metadata. The `safe_plan_hash_backfill` method only fills plan_hash on existing open entries during replay when the snapshot hash hasn't changed, but never on initial creation. The spec's acceptance criterion 'Lineage entries auto-populate plan_hash from the milestone snapshot when callers omit it' is NOT met. The diff comments justify this as 'the snapshot read races with persist_plan()' — this is a valid concern but the spec explicitly accepted this trade-off and required auto-population. Either the spec needs to be amended to match the conservative provenance-only approach, or the code needs to auto-populate plan_hash on new row creation as the spec requires.

details.message: backend invocation failed for contract 'plan_and_implement' via 'claude': Claude returned empty result with no structured_output (contract: plan_and_implement, stdout_len: 921, session_policy: ReuseIfAllowed)

details.body: In `unique_terminal_replay_match` (fs.rs ~line 2832), the contract violation when `requested_run_id.is_some() && requested_started_at.is_none()` is handled by logging a `tracing::warn` and returning `None`. The caller then maps `None` to a generic 'no matching task run' error, losing the diagnostic information about the contract violation. Issue 2 specifies 'Replace any debug_assert! guards on this with runtime checks that return errors in both debug and release builds.' The current approach technically prevents the wrong behavior but the error message seen by callers/operators is misleading — it says 'no matching task run' when the real problem is a violated invariant. Change the function signature to return `Result<Option<usize>, AppError>` so the contract violation can propagate as a distinct error, or at minimum change the tracing level from `warn` to `error` since this represents a programming bug in a caller, not a recoverable warning.

The `safe_plan_hash_backfill` function (fs.rs line 2608-2616) skips backfill when `snapshot_plan_hash_at_creation` is `None`, which means legacy open entries created before plan persistence was added will never get their `plan_hash` filled during completion, even though the current snapshot has the answer. The test `running_entry_created_before_plan_is_not_backfilled_on_revisit` explicitly asserts this behavior. However, Issue 3 acceptance criteria states 'Lineage entries auto-populate plan_hash from the milestone snapshot when callers omit it' — this is not true for existing legacy open entries with no provenance. Add a comment in the test and near the `safe_plan_hash_backfill` None branch explicitly documenting this as a deliberate deviation from the original issue spec, referencing the ambiguity rationale, so future reviewers don't re-open this as a regression.

`src/adapters/fs.rs:3044-3055` now auto-populates new rows from the live milestone snapshot whenever the caller omits `plan_hash`, and `src/adapters/fs.rs:3343-3355` later treats that stored value as authoritative. That still breaks lineage when the plan changes after dispatch but before `record_task_run_start` runs: a bead dispatched under v1 can be recorded as v2 if `persist_plan` advances the snapshot first, and finalization with the later-discovered true v1 hash then fails with a false `conflicting plan_hash` error. The new comment already describes this race, so the amendment is still incomplete. Add a regression test for `dispatch under v1 -> snapshot advances to v2 -> start without plan_hash -> complete with explicit v1`, and avoid stamping the current snapshot hash onto a new row unless it is known to match the dispatched attempt.

details.message: backend invocation failed for contract 'plan_and_implement' via 'claude': invalid Claude result JSON: expected value at line 1 column 1 (contract: plan_and_implement, result_len: 676)

details.body: In `unique_terminal_replay_match` (fs.rs:2838-2844), the contract violation guard logs `tracing::error!` and returns `None`, which causes the caller to return a generic `CorruptRecord` error ("no matching task run" or "ambiguous task run update"). Issue 2 requires "runtime checks that return errors" — the current path does return an error, but the error message seen by the caller describes data corruption rather than the actual programmer contract violation (run_id supplied without started_at). Consider returning an explicit `Err` from `unique_terminal_replay_match` (changing the return type to `AppResult<Option<usize>>`) so the contract violation surfaces as its own distinct error rather than being masked by a misleading CorruptRecord wrapper. This prevents future debugging confusion if the contract is ever violated.

In `src/adapters/fs.rs:3118-3176`, the `run_id` update path returns an error as soon as it sees a single open runless row whose `started_at` differs from the request. That means a replay of an older completed runless attempt is still rejected once a newer retry has started: the code never falls through to the terminal replay matcher at `src/adapters/fs.rs:3127-3152`, even if a completed row exists with the requested `started_at`. The accepted Issue 2 fix requires started-at-based disambiguation for terminal replays too; this branch still breaks the partial-write repair path described in `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:527-530`.

details.message: backend invocation failed for contract 'plan_and_implement' via 'claude': Claude returned empty result with no structured_output (contract: plan_and_implement, stdout_len: 923, session_policy: ReuseIfAllowed)

details.body: In `unique_terminal_replay_match`, the `AppError::CorruptRecord` error uses `file: "unique_terminal_replay_match"` (a function name) rather than a file path. All other `CorruptRecord` errors in fs.rs use actual file paths like `milestones/{id}/task-runs.ndjson`. This should use the task-runs.ndjson path for consistency and to help operators locate the problematic file. The caller has `milestone_id` available and could pass it in, or the function could accept it as a parameter.

details.message: backend invocation failed for contract 'plan_and_implement' via 'claude': schema validation failed for stage 'plan_and_implement': missing field `change_summary`

details.body: In `safe_plan_hash_backfill` (fs.rs ~line 3395), the call site in the `update_task_run` open-entry finalization path calls `safe_plan_hash_backfill` AFTER setting `entry.plan_hash` from the caller's explicit value (line 3389: `entry.plan_hash = Some(plan_hash.to_owned())`). This means `safe_plan_hash_backfill` will always short-circuit on `entry.plan_hash.is_some()` when the caller supplied a plan_hash. This is correct behavior. However, there is a subtle ordering issue: `safe_plan_hash_backfill` checks `entry.outcome.is_terminal()` to skip terminal entries, but it is called on line 3395 BEFORE `entry.outcome = outcome` is set on line 3396. Since this code path is inside the `else` branch (meaning the entry is NOT yet terminal — it's being transitioned TO terminal), the `is_terminal()` check will see the old Running outcome, which means the terminal guard in `safe_plan_hash_backfill` is vacuous here. The backfill still proceeds correctly because the entry is about to become terminal, and this is exactly when you'd want the last chance to backfill. Document this ordering dependency with a brief inline comment so future refactors don't reorder the calls and break the invariant.

The `unique_terminal_replay_match` contract enforcement (fs.rs ~line 2839) uses `AppError::CorruptRecord` for what is actually an internal programming invariant violation (caller passed `(Some(run_id), None)` for started_at). `CorruptRecord` implies corrupt on-disk data. Consider using a distinct error variant or at minimum adjust the error message prefix to say 'internal contract violation' rather than naming the ndjson file, since the file is not corrupt — the caller violated the API contract. This matters for error triage: an operator seeing 'CorruptRecord' in logs may investigate the data file rather than the calling code.

Issues 2 and 3 are not implemented as specified. The acceptance criteria require `run_id` and `plan_hash` to be required (`&str`, not `Option<&str>`) in `record_bead_start` and `update_task_run` at the service port level, and all runless matching/backfill and snapshot auto-population code to be removed. Instead, both parameters remain `Option<&str>` in `TaskRunLineagePort` (service.rs:103,117) and in `record_bead_start` (service.rs:~410). The runless matching code (`find_matching_running_task_run` runless fallback, `unique_terminal_replay_match` runless guards, the `[index] if entries[*index].run_id.is_none()` arm in update_task_run) was enhanced rather than removed. Similarly, `safe_plan_hash_backfill`, `snapshot_plan_hash`, and `snapshot_plan_hash_at_creation` were added rather than removed. The code contains a comment acknowledging 'Deliberate deviation from Issue 3 acceptance criteria' (fs.rs:2609). Either the spec must be amended to accept the refined-optional approach, or the implementation must be reworked to match the specified required-parameter approach. The current state satisfies neither — it claims to address the issues but implements the opposite of what was specified.

FsTaskRunLineageStore directly instantiates FsMilestoneSnapshotStore in `snapshot_plan_hash` (fs.rs:2555), bypassing the service layer's port abstraction. This creates a tight coupling between two adapters that should be independent. If a different MilestoneSnapshotPort implementation is used (e.g., for testing or a database backend), the lineage store would still read from the filesystem snapshot. The snapshot read should either be passed in by the caller (service layer) or the snapshot store should be injected as a dependency of the lineage store.

`unique_terminal_replay_match` (fs.rs:~2840) uses `AppError::CorruptRecord` for an internal programming error (caller passes `Some(run_id)` without `Some(started_at)`). The error message says 'This indicates a bug in the calling code, not corrupt data' but uses the CorruptRecord variant, which conflates API contract violations with actual data corruption during operator triage. Add a dedicated error variant (e.g., `AppError::InternalContractViolation`) or use `unreachable!()` / `debug_assert!` if this is truly a path that cannot be reached.

`run_id` is still not enforced as a required start/completion identity. `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:97-123` and `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:401-495` still expose `run_id: Option<&str>`, while `src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:473-541` and `src/adapters/fs.rs:2820-3331` still implement the old runless started-at fallback/backfill flow (`find_matching_running_task_run`, `matching_finalized_task_runs`, `unique_terminal_replay_match`). That means a bead can still be started without a run ID and later finalized by matching a runless row, which is exactly the ambiguity Issue 2 was supposed to remove. Make `run_id` required in the service/port APIs, keep `TaskRunEntry.run_id` optional only for serde compatibility, delete the runless matching/backfill branches, and remove or rewrite the legacy tests that still assert this behavior in `src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:1030-1217` and `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:4673-4724,5691-5749`.

`plan_hash` is still optional at bead start and the snapshot-derived backfill machinery is still active. `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:97-105` and `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:401-437` still allow `plan_hash: Option<&str>`, and `src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:286-292` plus `src/adapters/fs.rs:2570-2624,3020-3027,3052-3071,3403-3415` retain `snapshot_plan_hash_at_creation`, `snapshot_plan_hash`, and `safe_plan_hash_backfill`. That leaves lineage rows dependent on mutable snapshot state instead of the controller’s dispatch-time hash, which is the behavior Issue 3 explicitly asked to remove. Make `plan_hash` required in `record_bead_start`, drop the snapshot provenance/backfill fields and helpers, and rewrite the tests that currently codify the rejected contract in `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:4727-4819,5392-5962`.

details.message: backend invocation failed for contract 'plan_and_implement' via 'claude': schema validation failed for stage 'plan_and_implement': missing field `order`

details.body: In `find_matching_running_task_run` (model.rs:467) and `matching_finalized_task_runs` (model.rs:485), the `_started_at: DateTime<Utc>` parameter is now completely unused (prefixed with underscore). These functions match solely by `run_id`. The parameter should be removed from the function signatures and all call sites (fs.rs:2821, fs.rs:2854) to avoid misleading callers into thinking `started_at` plays a role in matching. Leaving dead parameters in public functions creates a maintenance trap — future developers may assume the parameter is load-bearing and try to 'fix' bugs by adjusting the timestamp they pass.

The `collapse_task_run_attempts` pipeline (model.rs:499-573) retains full legacy runless matching: `find_collapse_group_index` (line 516-530) falls through to `unique_open_legacy_group_index` when `entry.run_id` is `None`, and `legacy_group_accepts_completion` (line 564-573) matches terminal entries to open legacy starts by `started_at`. While these are needed for backward-compatible reads of old data, the Issue 2 acceptance criteria states 'all runless matching/backfill code is removed.' Either (a) add a code comment block explaining that collapse is a read-only backward-compat path deliberately retained for legacy data, or (b) if the project truly has no legacy data, remove the functions. Without annotation, a future reviewer will flag the same gap.

In `update_task_run` (fs.rs:2960-3015), when the exact `run_id` match returns zero results (the `[]` arm at line 2961), the code falls through to search for open entries WITHOUT filtering by run_id (lines 2962-2968 filter only for non-terminal, non-superseded entries). If a single such open entry is found with a *different* run_id, the code returns an error (line 2999-3005) — but if zero open entries are found, it calls `unique_terminal_replay_match` (line 2982) which correctly requires run_id match. The intermediate fallback to `open_matches` without run_id filtering is confusing: for the `[index]` arm (single open entry with different run_id), the error message is generated but the path where `open_matches` has multiple entries (the `_` arm at line 3007) does not mention the run_id mismatch, producing a less helpful 'ambiguous' error. Consider adding the mismatched run_id(s) to the error message in the multi-open-match arm for debuggability.

Add a migration or one-time compatibility repair for persisted running task-run rows that still have `run_id: null`. `src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:462-477` and `src/adapters/fs.rs:2954-3005` now require an exact run-id match to finalize a row, so an in-flight legacy start from the previous build can no longer be completed after upgrade. Retrying start is not neutral either: `src/adapters/fs.rs:2893-2909` marks the legacy row failed and appends a new attempt. Without an upgrade step, mid-flight milestones from older versions will be stranded or misreported as failed.
